calendar.matters:
Petition For Order: (1) Removing Trustee; (2) Compelling Trustee To Account; (3) Reduction Of Trustee Compensation; (4) Barring Trustee?S Use Of Trust Funds For Litigation; And (5) Awarding Damages And Attorneys? Fees And Costs For Financial Elder Abuse
Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case no. PTR-22-305483, Etta Bacharach Testamentary Trust.

Appearances via Zoom: James Cummins, Esq. and Chelsea Suttman, Esq. for Sheila Siegel and Suzanne Borsuk (Petitioners). Tad Devlin, Esq. and Emmalee McCarthy, Esq. for Mark Borsuk (Respondent/Trustee).

Mr. Cummins stated that the petition was filed in the estate case no. PES-06-178094 and also filed in the trust case no. PTR-22-305483. The Court ordered the same petition in case no. PES-06-178094 off calendar.

Mr. Cummins argued in support of suspending the trustee stating that their request for information dating back to March is still not satisfied and that there is a lack of information to the beneficiaries. Mr. Cummins stated that the trustee should be suspended, the he should be ordered to account in 60 days, that the matter should be set for trial, and that there should be a removal, a surcharge, and a fiduciary appointed by the Court. Mr. Devlin stated that they are just joining the case, that the trustee is being fully transparent and actively handling affairs, and that there has been no use of estate funds. Mr. Devlin also stated that there are active discussions about mediation but this is not as simple as a division of property, that they are in the process of preparing written responses, that they are responding to counsel's prior request for information, that there is no co-mingling, and that the removal is not needed. The Court inquired about a distribution in kind/distribution of shares. Mr. Devlin stated that it could be discussed in mediation. Mr. Cummins stated that the accounting should be code compliant, that it is not based on the date of death, that the trust holds nine income generating properties, that they want date-of-death appraisals, and that they are necessary for the estate tax returns. The Court inquired about what's required under the code. Brief discussions are held. Mr. Cummins stated that they need to see the transactions in the other accounts, that there is an issue as to the Chestnut property, and that the trustee is using his real estate license for self-interest. The Court stated that there are no issues of fact at this point, that everyone should take a fresh approach to this, that this is a new opportunity, and that it wants counsel to meet and confer on ADR prior to the next calling of the matter.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court ordered the trustee to provide an account for the two-year period of: 2/7/19 through 2/6/21. The accounting is to be code compliant. The Court denied Mr. Cummins request for the trustee to provide an accounting for his full term. The Court noted that it appreciates what has been added here today and ordered counsel to meet and confer on ADR/how to proceed on the pending petition.

The Petition for Order 1) Removing Trustee & Surcharge 2) Compelling Trustee to Account 3) Reducing Trustee's Use of Trust Funds for Litigation and 5) Awarding Damages and Attorney's Fee and Costs for Financial Elder Abuse is continued to March 1, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Status on the filing of the accounting is also set for March 1, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.
